title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
summary: Google DeepMind presented Gemini 3 Flash Preview, which provides audio understanding including speaker identification, timestamps, emotion detection, language detection with translation, and summarization through a single API call. The model also supports speech generation directed by text descriptions and enables real-time multimodal audio processing.
writeup: A Google DeepMind developer-experience engineer walks through the current state of AI audio at DeepMind, covering three distinct capabilities built on Gemini 3 Flash: rich audio understanding (speaker diarization, emotion tagging, multilingual transcription in one API call), directive speech synthesis (a small set of base voices steerable by scene description and director-style notes), and Gemini 2.0 Flash Live, a native sound-to-sound real-time multimodal model. The through-line is that a shared audio-understanding substrate powers all three tiers, so the same comprehension that parses pacing and accent in transcription also enables accent-accurate synthesis and in-model reasoning without a text-LLM intermediary.

similar.title: NEW Gemini Features Explained — How to Use Google’s Latest AI Upgrade
Google strikes back! Gemini Omni, AI glasses, new models
From prompt to a fully working real-time voice app in seconds. 🎙️✨
Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind

key_points: EchoScript, a demo app in the Google AI Studio gallery, extracts speaker labels, timestamps, language tags, emotion labels (happy, sad, angry, neutral), and an overall summary from a single Gemini 3 Flash Preview API call with a structured JSON response schema.

Rather than maintaining a large voice library filtered by gender or accent, Gemini speech generation starts from roughly 30 base voices and accepts a natural-language director's note (scene setting, performance guidance, transcript) to steer accent and delivery, demonstrated by converting a standard American base voice to a convincing Irish brogue and Singaporean Singlish.

Gemini 2.0 Flash Live is a native audio-to-audio model that accepts real-time text, audio, and video over a WebSocket connection and returns streamed audio plus a text transcript, with reasoning and intelligence baked into the audio model itself rather than routed through a separate text LLM.

All three capabilities share a common foundation in Gemini 3 foundational research, meaning the model genuinely understands what different accents, pacing patterns, and emotional registers sound like rather than applying post-hoc filtering.
Google AI Studio (ai.google.dev/aistudio) lets developers try Gemini Live and the voice library without a paid API key, making it a zero-cost entry point for audio model evaluation.
